text:
Once upon a time, there was a small moonlit forest. It was beautiful and serene. The squirrels chattered away to themselves and the owls hummed softly. In the center of this tranquility dwelt the wise old owl. One afternoon he decided to take a nap.

Asleep, he didn't notice a fluffy white tail gliding through. The fox paused and watched curiously as the owl slept peacefully. When he finally fell asleep, the fox couldn't help himself and pounced. The owl awoke startled.

"Well, you might as well be peaceful," he grumbled, shaking himself. "What have you got?"

"A gift."

"The fox?"

"Oh. Yeah."

The fox handed over a beautiful white feather. "What good are feathers?"

"Well," the owl pondered. "They might get you noticed."

"A wise choice." The fox stroked the owl's plumage. "You know?"

"Not really. That's just my hat."

"The owl hat."
